products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Kapuas
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Kapuas

Bucephalandra Kapuas is a compact epiphyte featuring dark, moody foliage and a subtle metallic sheen. This Buce variant displays narrow, ruffled oval-shaped leaves that range from deep green to brownish hues, often developing richer coloration under stronger lighting. Ideal for nano tanks, shrimp setups, and detailed hardscape work, Bucephalandra Kapuas adds texture and depth without overwhelming a layout. A reliable choice for aquascapers looking for hardy structure and understated color contrast.

Like other epiphytic aquatic plants, Kapuas grows from a rhizome and should be attached to rock or driftwood. It thrives in low to medium light and does well in both low-tech aquascapes and CO2-injected aquariums. While growth is slow, stable water parameters and regular fertilization will encourage fuller, healthier clumps over time.

Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant, and prefers to be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not cover the rhizome when planting the Bucephalandra in substrate. This will cause melt and rot.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the rockwool surrounding the roots and attach it to the hardscape. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • Bucephalandra’s final size, coloration, and growth rate are dependent upon the condition of the aquarium.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters can result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Araceae
Endemic To: Borneo
Leaves: Small to medium (1-2 inches)
pH: 5.5-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
Co2: Not necessary, but recommended
Propagation: Cut by rhizome
Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Bucephalandra Artemis
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Artemis

Bucephalandra ‘Artemis’ is a striking and relatively rare aquatic plant, prized in the aquascaping hobby for its unique dark green tones with subtle iridescent highlights. Its palm tree-like leaves are small, elongated, and slightly wavy, often showing a silvery sheen when grown in moderate to high light, giving the foreground or midground a touch of “glow” in the planted tank.

Like most Buce, it grows slowly and prefers being attached to hardscape like driftwood or rocks rather than being planted directly in substrate. It benefits from stable water parameters, and while Bucephalandra doesn’t demand CO2 injection and can thrive in low-tech aquariums, adding it can encourage faster growth and more vibrant coloration.

Notes:

products.title: Anchor Moss on Stainless Steel
products.body_html:
Common Name: Anchor Moss, Vesicularia

Anchor Moss is a very densely ramified, bushy moss with a soft, delicate texture, similar to needles of a pine tree. Its dense stems are broad and shoot out at nearly right angles. Planting moss in the aquarium foreground or midground provides compact softness, especially in smaller tanks. As one of the lighter-colored green aquarium mosses, it tends to brightly stand out and add contrast to any brown or grey surfaces.

This aquatic moss can grow in both emersed and submerged conditions, and attaches well to substrate, driftwood, or rocks. While it's known to grow slowly, Anchor Moss has a wide range of light tolerance. The greater the light, the darker the moss will grow.

Notes:

  • Aquarium moss is extremely hardy and will survive in a wide range of aquarium parameters.
  • Please note that most moss species can contain brown bits, and while suitable for low tech tanks, CO2 injection will yield better growth and coloration.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Hypnaceae

Origin: Singapore

Height: 3.9” – 7.9”

pH: 5 - 7

Care: Medium

Light: Low to High

Co2: Medium

Propagation: Dividing off daughter plants.

Growth Rate: Slow

products.title: Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa
products.body_html:
Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa is an interesting species of Ludwigia that is native to the United States and commonly found in moist, wet coastal areas along Massachusetts down to eastern Texas. Unlike other species in the Ludwigia genus, Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa is not characterized by quick-growing stems. Instead, Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa is a suitable choice for the foreground and midground area of planted aquariums. With consistent pruning, this aquarium plant displays attractive crowns that grow low and range in red to orange colors. Unlike the usual vertical growth, this particular species grows low and exhibits horizontal growth patterns. To maintain this aquatic plant, relatively strong aquarium lighting should be used and this plant should not be placed in areas that receive shade. 

Doing so results in a dull green color along with slower growth. Like other stem plants, propagation is simple. Cut the stem and replant into quality aquarium soil.  

Notes:

  • Ludwigia can be very fragile. Be sure to handle this aquarium plant with care.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Genus: Ludwigia

Family Name: Onagraceae

Origin: Southeastern U.S.

Height: 4-12”

pH: 4.5 - 7.0

Care: Easy

Light: Medium to High

Co2: Recommended

Propagation: Cuttings

Growth Rate: Fast

products.body_html:
Common Name: Blood Vomit

Centrolepis Drummondiana is a rare foreground aquarium plant with fine, grassy blades and subtle reddish tones at the base under strong light. It stays compact and low, making it a great choice for detailed planted tank layouts where texture matters. Best suited for high-tech aquariums, this plant rewards stable conditions with a unique look you won’t see in most fish tanks.

This species prefers bright lighting & consistent CO2 to maintain tight growth and healthy, vibrant color. Growth is on the slower side, so patience pays off, especially in well-maintained aquascape layouts where it won’t be shaded by taller stem plants.

products.title: Eriocaulon Feather Duster
products.body_html:
Add delicate texture and movement to your aquascape with Eriocaulon ‘Feather Duster.’ This aquatic plant forms soft, feather-like blades that create a natural, elegant midground or background display. Its fine leaves respond beautifully to bright light and nutrients, giving your tank a lush, detailed look.

Eriocaulon ‘Feather Duster’ is a grassy aquarium plant ideal for aquascapers who enjoy a patient, rewarding planting experience. It thrives when paired with CO2 and regular fertilization, forming compact clumps that enhance both contrast and depth in your layout. With proper care, it brings a unique softness to any freshwater aquascape.

products.title: Tree Moss
products.body_html:
Climacium dendroides, often called “Tree Moss,” is a unique moss with upright, branching growth that resembles tiny forest trees. Its dense, textured appearance adds a vertical element to terrariums or vivariums, creating natural-looking miniature landscapes.

This moss thrives in moist, shaded environments with stable humidity and can be attached to rocks, wood, or soil. This hardy terrestrial plant is ideal for new or experienced aquascapers looking to add intricate detail and depth to foregrounds or midgrounds while maintaining a low maintenance, long-lasting accent.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Occasional trimming will help maintain its shape and encourage denser growth.
  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary as this is a live plant.
  • Additional research should be done to provide optimum growing conditions for the plant to thrive.

products.title: Hygrophila Salicifolia Narrow Leaf
products.body_html:
Hygrophila salicifolia is an aquarium plant that is great for all skill levels. Often compared to Water Wisteria, this fast-growing Hygro is just as hardy while offering a more upright growth habit, making it an excellent choice for filling out the background of a planted aquarium. Like many aquatic plants, it may experience some initial melt as it adjusts to a new tank. Give it time to establish healthy roots and it will quickly rebound. Once settled, Hygrophila salicifolia grows vigorously and adapts well to a wide range of water conditions.

As long as aquarium parameters remain reasonably stable, this plant will continue producing new growth with minimal effort. In lower light setups, expect slightly leggier growth, while higher light and CO2 injection will produce thicker stems and faster development. If you're looking for a hardy, fast-growing stem plant, this is a great one to try.

Key Features & Important Notes: 

  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary and should be expected with live plants.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

products.title: Marcgravia Sintenisii
products.body_html:
Common Name: Rainbow Marcgravia

Marcgravia sintenisii is a stunning tropical vine from the rainforests of South America. Its shingling leaves showcase a captivating gradient, shifting from red to orange to green, adding a vibrant splash of color to any terrarium or paludarium. The vines naturally climb toward the light, creating graceful, vertical displays.

This terrestrial plant thrives in low to medium light and prefers its roots and growing surfaces to stay slightly moist, while avoiding prolonged water on the leaves. With the right care, Marcgravia sintenisii rewards plant enthusiasts with striking coloration and elegant, cascading growth.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Shingling plants do best in environments with vertical surfaces to climb.
  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary as this is a live plant.
  • Additional research on Marcgravia sintenisii care should be done to provide optimum growing conditions for the plant to thrive.

products.title: Umbrella Moss
products.body_html:
Common Name: Giant Rose Moss

Rhodobryum giganteum is a unique moss that forms miniature rosettes resembling tiny, delicate roses. Native to the humid forests of Asia, this slow-growing moss thrives in high-humidity environments and adds a lush, ornamental touch to terrariums & paludariums. Each rosette gradually expands into dense clusters, creating a soft, textured carpet that accentuates rocks, driftwood, or substrate surfaces.

Ideal for hobbyists seeking a unique, low-maintenance moss, Umbrella Moss prefers moderate indirect light & consistent moisture. With proper care, it becomes a standout feature in any humid display, offering both elegance and a natural, forest-like aesthetic.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • Each rosette typically grows about 0.5–1.5 cm in diameter. Clusters can slowly expand over time.
  • Attach to driftwood or rock to prevent rot.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive. Size, shape and color may vary as this is a live plant.
  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.

products.title: Pellionia Repens
products.body_html:
Common Name: Trailing Watermelon Begonia, Watermelon Vine, Elatostema repens, Pellionia repens, Rainbow vine

Pellionia Repens, commonly named Trailing Watermelon Begonia, is an evergreen perennial that is great for at providing ground cover. Although called a Begonia, Pellionia Repens is not related to Begonias and is part of the Urticaceae family. The ovate leaves of the Watermelon Vine are dark green with randomly scattered patterns of light green to silver. Under more light the stems will become a pinkish red color and the leaves will develop a maroon tint.

Pellionia Repens thrive in high humidity environments and should be in soil that are constantly moist. Leaves may begin to brown if the humidity is low. Originating from tropical forest floors and understories, Pellionia Repens does not tolerate cold or high light well. Having a vine like growth pattern, Pellionia Repens can be planted in terrestrial setups, such as terrariums and paladariums to provide great coverage.

Key Features & Important Notes: 

  • Pellionia Repens is a terrestrial plant and being submerged will result in death of the plant
  • Pellionia Repens prefers being grown with high humidity, partial shade, and moist soil.
  • Watermelon Begonia is not actually a Begonia.
  • Plants may grow differently depending on the environment in which they are grown in.
  • Additional research should be done to provide optimum growing conditions for the plant to thrive.

Family Name: Urticaceae
Origin: Southeast Asia
Temperature: 40-80 F
Care: Easy
Light: Shaded Light 
Water: Medium
Propagation: Herbaceous stem cutting 
Growth rate: Medium

Like all moss species, Taiwan Moss is extremely easy to care for and will adapt to a wide range of aquarium parameters and water conditions. Taiwan Moss has the capability to live in cold water tanks along with tropical aquariums. It doesn't require too much lighting and is a great hiding spot for small freshwater shrimp species or fish fry. It can be a slow grower, but if given the chance, is a beautiful addition a planted aquarium. It can be attached to hardscape material using glue, thread or ties and will eventually attach itself with time providing a beautiful aged look.

products.title: Ranalisma Rostratum
products.body_html:
Common Name: Ranalisma Rostratum

Ranalisma Rostratum originates from the tropics in Asia. This aquatic plant is still very rare in the trade, difficult to get and is often spelled incorrectly making it more elusive to eager planted tank enthusiasts. This aquatic plant is peculiar in that when it is grown submerged, it appears completely different and its leaves will transform into very narrow blades that resemble a grass-like plant. It features a bright green color and is comparable to the popular aquatic plant, Helanthium Parvulum. When emersed, Ranalisma Rostratum will have much rounder leaves and can sprout small flowers that bear small spiky fruits. In order to grow this plant correctly, high lighting, CO2 injection and a nutrient dense substrate are some of the basic requirements for success. If these factors are provided, this aquatic plant will grow a nice dense lawn of green that will be a beautiful addition to a planted aquarium.

Family Name: Alismataceae

Origin: Asia

Height: 2-5"

pH: 6-7.5

Care: Medium

Light: Medium

Co2: Not Required

Propagation: Replant runners

Growth rate: Slow to Moderate

products.title: Echinodorus Palaefolius
products.body_html:
Common Name: Echinodorus Palaefolius

Echinodorus Palaefolius is naturally found in marshy type conditions. It features rounded leaves, but when submerged, this aquatic plant sports leaves that are a bit more narrow and lengthy. Like most swords, this variant is also a heavy root feeder and should be planted in a nutrient-rich aquarium substrate to provide optimal conditions. If planted in an inert substrate such as sand or gravel, care should be taken to provide another form of nutritious supplement whether via root tabs or a quality plant fertilizer. Newer leaves may produce a slight reddish tint and will fully color up as the plant matures. It has quite a few trade names and is often mistaken as some of the following: Latifolius, Echinodorus Rigidifolius, Echinodorus Subalatus, and Echinodorus Cordifolius.

With a nutrient rich substrate these plants will tolerate most lighting conditions, but moderate to high lighting is ideal. CO2 injection is not necessary but will help the plant grow faster if desired. Also a fast grower, regular pruning will be required to allow light filtration to lower levels of the plant. Echinodorus Palaefolius will grow submersed or emersed. The emersed form has the potential to produce lovely white flowers.

Notes:

  • Echinodorus is a very hardy species of aquatic plants. Most Echinodorus require nutrient-dense soil to thrive as they are heavy root feeders.
  • Echinodorus grow fairly large and are more suitable for larger planted aquariums.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Alismataceae

Origin: South America

Height: 4-20”

pH: 6.5-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Moderate

Co2: Not necessary

Propagation: Adventitious shoots

Growth rate: Fast

products.title: Echinodorus Impai
products.body_html:
Amazon sword plants are very common in the aquarium hobby, being that they are beautiful and easy to care for. They offer excellent and attractive coverage to the background of an aquascape. These plants have short stems that grow in a rosette, bearing long and ovoid leaves with a pointed tip. Their leaves are dark or bright green in color with prominent veins and slightly wavy shape.

Amazon sword plants can get pretty huge, making them a great fit for a large tank. Their large leaves can easily restrict light to other plants if they are not pruned regularly. These plants propagate through side shoots off of their leaves, which can be trimmed and replanted into the substrate. To control the size of your Amazon sword plant you can remove the side shoots and older leaves.

With a nutrient rich substrate these plants will tolerate most lighting conditions, but moderate to high lighting is ideal. CO2 injection is not necessary but will help the plant grow faster if desired. Amazon sword plants will grow submersed or emersed. Emersed plants produce lovely white flowers.

products.title: Dymax eZ Filter
products.body_html:
The Dymax eZ Filter is an efficient and well performing internal filter that will help maintain proper aquarium health. Filter media within the filter have a high surface area, making it perfect for beneficial bacteria to colonize and thrive. The eZ Filter has all its media separated with a filter basket and includes white wool (mechanical filtration), activated carbon (chemical filtration), and biological sponge (biological filter). Filter media should be cleaned or replaced regularly to maintain optimum performance.

The Dymax eZ Filter is powered by a powerful Dymax pump. It will draw water from both the top and bottom levels of the water column, and water will be expelled towards the middle. This internal filter is suitable for both freshwater and marine aquariums. The Dymax IQ Skimmer can be attached onto the Dymax eZ Filter for marine aquariums so that protein skimming and filtering can all be done by one unit. This internal filter is suitable for tank sizes 60-90cm in length and 30cm in height.

Aquarium water should be used when cleaning filter media to ensure that the beneficial bacteria is not killed.

products.body_html:
Spathiphyllum Wallisii, more commonly known as a Peace Lily is a marsh plant originating from Central America. Peace Lilies are not true aquatic plants and it is not recommended to permanently submerge the plant. Spathiphyllum Wallisii will do best when grown in a terrarium or paludarium setup where its roots can stay moist. It requires medium to high lighting and is a relatively slow grower. If taken care of well, Peace Lilies can bloom twice a year and their flowers are a beautiful sight to see. Propagation is also simple and can be done by separating the rhizome and replanting. Although this plant is not a true lily and is not poisonous, care should be taken when handling as it can cause some discomfort if ingested. Keep this plant out of the reach of children and pets as well!

products.title: Bucephalandra Sanggau UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Buce Sanggau

Bucephalandra Sanggau is a variety of Buce that features variable color and slightly ruffled leaves. This type of aquatic plant is great for adding extra texture and color to midground areas of an aquascape. Like other Buce, it is relatively hardy and will adapt to a range of water conditions. As a baseline, we recommend adding Buce to established tanks for better transition and less melt. Cultivated through tissue culture, Bucephalandra Sanggau is free from pests and contaminants, ensuring a healthy start in your aquarium. Its low maintenance requirements make it suitable for both beginners and seasoned aquarists. Whether attached to driftwood, rocks, or planted in the substrate, this plant adds natural beauty to any freshwater setup.

Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant, so it can be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not plant Bucephalandra in the substrate and cover the rhizome. This will cause it to melt and rot.
  • Bucephalandra’s final size, coloration and growth rate are dependent upon the condition of the aquarium Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting.  Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.

Family Name: Araceae

Endemic To: Borneo

pH: 6-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Low to Medium

Co2: Not necessary but recommended 

Propagation: Cut by rhizome

Growth rate: Slow

Celebrate the season by adding a festive touch to your aquarium with one or two live moss trees. Featuring lush Christmas Moss carefully pre-wrapped, this piece is a fun seasonal accent for your planted tank or terrarium.

Christmas Moss is a low-light, low-maintenance choice that’s easy for hobbyists of all experience levels to keep. With proper care and patience, it will continue to grow and fill out naturally. Simply place the tree into your aquascape. As the moss grows, new trimmings can be cut and reattached to rocks or driftwood.

Notes:

  • Aquarium moss is extremely hardy and will survive in a wide range of aquarium parameters.
  • Please note, most moss species can contain brown bits and while suitable for low tech tanks, CO2 injection will yield better growth and coloration.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Care: Easy
Light: Low
Co2: Not necessary
Propagation: Separate
Growth rate: Moderate

products.title: Twinstar Sterilizer for Fish
products.body_html:
Twinstar Yotta is a powerful sterilizer that aid in the prevention of common fish diseases. These products work by eliminating pathogens found in the water column along with mold spores that may find their way into the aquarium. The Twinstar Yotta is designed to have no impact on biological balances within the aquarium tank and are safe to use with tropical fish.

Fish disease is commonly caused by a mix of 3 factors. These factors include the environment, basic fish immunity, and pathogens. If the environment is compromised and immunity is low, the presence of pathogens and risk increases. Twinstar sterilizer models for fish effectively eliminate fish disease through strong sterilizing factors, so it doesn’t need to run 24 hours like UV sterilizer. This device will not harm aerobic bacteria and helps to kill pathogens, so it’s safe. On top of this, Twinstar's lifespan is triple that of general UV sterilizers! 

Please note, this device will not cure existing ailments and is intended to be used for prevention. It is recommended to use in combination with good husbandry for best results.

products.title: Selaginella Kraussiana Aurea
products.body_html:
Selaginella Kraussiana 'Aurea', is a low-growing terrarium plant that's great for ground coverage in terrariums, vivariums or gardens. This variety forms dense, feathery carpets that add vibrant color to any setup. Commonly compared to mosses and ferns, Selaginella displays characteristics of both! Its unruly growth habit creates attractive shingling that spreads slowly, also making it manageable in comparison to faster-growing plants. For best results, Selaginella does well in consistently humid environments with partially shaded lighting. It doesn't require much to root and is a great low-maintenance choice if you're searching for unique coverage and texture.

Notes:

  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Selaginella Kraussiana works well in terrarium-like setups and should be partially shaded with good humidity.
  • Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into quality soil. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here.
  • Yellowing of leaves indicates overwatering.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Lycopodiaceae
Origin: Africa
Height: 1-8”
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
Growth rate: Slow to Moderate

products.title: Hygrophila sp. Chai UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Hygrophila sp. Chai is an extremely rare mutation of the well-known Hygrophila lancea ‘Araguaia’, and remains one of the more elusive plants in the aquarium hobby. Due to its rarity and limited cultivation, available quantities are typically limited. This unique aquarium plant features slender leaves with signature olive-red tones that can develop vivid pink tones under certain conditions. Chai grows in a compact, creeping habit similar to standard Araguaia.

Unlike typical aquarium stem plants, Hygrophila Araguaia sp. Chai grows in short, bushy clusters, making it well suited for use as a foreground-to-midground accent. It works especially well in nature-style and Dutch-inspired layouts where texture and color variation are key. Propagation is straightforward once established and can be achieved by trimming lateral shoots or cutting along the stem and replanting. As with other Hygrophila varieties, consistent lighting, nutrient-rich substrate, and CO2 injection will yield the best coloration and growth.

Family Name: Lythraceae
Origin: Singapore
Height: 2-5”
Care: Medium to Difficult
Light: High
Co2: Required
Propagation: Cuttings
Growth Rate: Fast

products.title: Eriocaulon sp Quinquangulare Red UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Eriocaulon Quinquangulare Red is a rare and coveted Erio. Still uncommon within the aquarium hobby, this aquatic plant is best suited for experienced aquascapers. For proper growht, it requires strong lighting, nutrient-rich aquarium soil and consistent CO2 injection. It should be planted in mature tanks that experience stable parameters. When grown successfully, Eriocaulon Quinquangulare is a beautiful ornamental plant that's perfect for the midground of planted aquariums.

It is easily a focal plant thanks to its narrow, spiky leaves that feature a gradient—deep reddish tones at the base that gradually fade into lighter green toward the tips. Its commonly cultivated in emersed forms for improved resilience, but can be transitioned to submerged growth when provided with stable conditions and careful attention. Propagation can be done by gently dividing healthy, mature plants that are established.

Genus: Eriocaulon
Family Name: Eriocaulaceae
Origin: Vietnam
Height: 3-5 inches
Care: Difficult
Lighting: High
CO2: Required

products.title: Anubias Nana Ovalis
products.body_html:
Common Name: Anubias Nana Ovalis 

Anubias Nana "Ovalis" is a less common variation of Anubias that makes a beautiful midground plant. The plant is tall with large, dark green, soft and rounded wavy leaves. These plants are hardy and will accept a wide range of water parameters, making them perfect for beginners to the hobby. This plant can be grown either submersed or emersed, and works very well in bare-bottomed tanks.

Anubias Nana “Ovalis” plants prefer a tank with good water circulation. Since they are slow growing they are prone to algae, especially under high light. These plants prefer low to moderate lighting. They can survive without CO2 injection, but will grow faster and larger with it.

The rhizomes will attach to a rock, piece of driftwood, or other tank decoration, but they can also float or be planted. You can tie the plant down with thread or fishing line to encourage it to root in a specific spot. Anubias Nana “Ovalis” propagates through its rhizome system.

  • Anubias are strong plants that can thrive in a variety of environments.
  • These epiphytes can be planted above the substrate or attached to hardscape.
  • Burying the rhizome, will cause the plant to melt and die.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Araceae

Origin: West Africa

Height: 1.5-4”

pH: 5.0-9.0

Care: Easy

Light: Low to medium

Co2: Not necessary but recommended

Propagation: Rhizome division

Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Barclaya Longifolia
products.body_html:
Common Name: Barclaya Longifolia

**Photo shows a bulb portion after 2 months of growth**

Barclaya is a rather uncommon, rare bulbaceous aquatic plant. Its long red leaves can grow into a large and stunning specimen when planted along with green stems and carpet plants. Under high light and with the addition of CO2 and fertilizers, the leaves can get a deep maroon red color. Although it can grow in lower light, the leaves tend to be drabber and will turn bronze colored. 

Regarding the bulb portion, all bulbs vary in size (0.5-1.5") but do not be deterred by its size! The bulb will continue to grow larger once properly planted. The leaves will sprout and grow to be around 8-12".

products.title: Pacific Driftwood Stump
products.body_html:
Pacific Driftwood Stumps

Driftwood stumps are perfect for foreground placement. It provides a sturdy base for aquascaping and can also be wrapped in moss! Alternatively, pair these pieces with your favorite Buce or Anubias. Their porous surface makes it easy for epiphytes to attach and grow. Pacific Wood looks like Malaysian driftwood's cousin! This natural aquascaping material shares a similar texture and surface, making it a top pick for aquascapers searching for a main wood. 

Size Specifications
Photos provided are a representation of what you will receive. As Pacific Wood Wood is a natural product, coloration, shape and size differences will occur and are to be expected. 

  • Dimensions: 5 - 10+"
  • Tank Size: 10+ gallons

Notes:

  • Driftwood can leech tannins which can cause a slight yellow or brown tint to your water. This is natural and the effects will reduce over time. Boiling the wood prior to use can decrease the tannins leeching.  
  • Pacific Wood is denser than other types, but all aquarium driftwood is buoyant and will float. Please ensure your piece is fully waterlogged prior to use in a planted tank or it will float upon filling the aquarium.
  • A large stone or heavy decor may be placed on top to ensure a non-water logged piece of wood stays submerged.
  • Important note: We recommend rinsing wood thoroughly before adding it to an established aquarium. If you prefer, you may also boil the wood for at least 1-2 hours to help release tannins.

products.title: Aquario Neo Booster Plants Ferti - Water Conditioner
products.body_html:
Aquario Neo Booster Plants Ferti is a water conditioner designed to boost aquatic plant growth and health without added CO2. Enriched with natural amino acids and organic carbon, this water conditioner supports improved nutrient absorption and photosynthesis resulting in stronger, more vibrant growth. Through this type of support, plants are able to readily absorb available nutrients, indirectly reducing algae growth.

This product is safe to use with or without your existing fertilizer routine and CO2 system! It provides a source of bioavailable organic carbon that plants need to grow properly. Typically obtained from CO2 injection, this solution provides it in a simple form that is required for photosynthesis and that won't interfere with existing CO2 systems. For best results, pair it with other Aquario Neo Water Conditioners and good tank maintenance habits.

Usage and Notes:
One capful (5ml) treats 200 Liters of tap water (approximately 52 gallons). When dosing directly into the tank, use it according to the capacity of the tank. If treating water that is added, dose according to the volume of water being added to the tank. 

  • Contains natural ingredients and may have an odor
  • 1 capful = 5ml on the 300 ml bottles
  • 1 capful = 10ml on the 1000 ml bottles
  • This liquid conditioner is not clear and may have a cloudy appearance initially, but will clear up in your aquarium shortly after usage
  • Shake before using
  • This product is for aquarium use only, keep away from children and pets

Sizes:
300 ml and 1000 ml bottles

featured_image.alt: Aquario Neo Booster Plants Ferti - Water Conditioner
Aquario Neo Booster Plants Ferti - Water Conditioner

products.title: Aquario Neo C Plants - Water Conditioner
products.body_html:
Aquario Neo C Plants is a specialized water conditioner designed for planted aquariums. Its formula neutralizes chlorine and heavy metals while supporting overall tank and aquatic plant health. Formulated with Neo Essence and Neo Booster Plants, this water conditioner contains organic carbon, natural amino acids, and chelating agents that support nutrient uptake, helping aquatic plants grow faster, strong, and with richer color. While an improvement in growth will be noticeable, Neo C Plants itself is not a fertilizer. Instead, it supports the ability to absorb nutrients already readily available in the water.

Beyond plant benefits, Neo C Plants promotes a balanced environment by supporting beneficial bacteria, improving fish digestion and reducing oxidative stress. Natural humic acid in the formula boosts livestock activity and bacterial performance, while natural minerals and betaine contribute trace elements and digestive support for resilience. This product is safe to use with your existing fertilizer routine and CO2 system!

Usage and Notes:

products.title: Aquario Neo Solution Complex - Liquid Fertilizer
products.body_html:
Aquario Neo Solution Complex is a complete, all-in-one fertilizer for planted aquariums. It provides key nutrients such as nitrogen, potassium, phosphate, and magnesium, while also supplying essential trace elements like iron, boron, molybdenum, and calcium to support healthy, balanced aquatic plant growth. Its formula is enriched with Neo Booster Plants, enhancing nutrient absorption and promoting more vibrant coloration. This convenient solution combines the benefits of Neo Solution 1 and 2 in a single product.

Dosage & Directions

  • 1 capful (on 300ml bottles) = 5ml
  • Shake well before use.
  • Dose 2 caps (10ml) per 10 gallons (40L) every 3 days.
  • Reduce dose by half following major trims.

Important Notes

  • For aquarium use only.
  • May settle, separate or have a slight smell. This is normal and does not indicate defective or expired product.
  • Keep out of reach of children and pets.
  • Do not use with other liquid fertilizers.

products.title: Chihiros Doctor 5
products.body_html:
The Chihiros Doctor 5 is a compact, smart device that helps keep your aquarium healthy by combining algae control, temperature monitoring, and remote app control in one unit. Its built-in display lets you check readings at a glance, while a magnetic connector between the replacement cartridge and main unit makes maintenance quick, secure, and waterproof.

Using micro-electrolysis technology, the Doctor 5 generates powerful oxidizing agents that target harmful algae and bacteria, helping to improve water clarity and lower the risk of disease for your fish — all without harsh chemicals. With three operating modes (Plant, Fish, Shrimp) and easy Bluetooth connectivity via the MyChihiros app, this all-in-one tool supports the well-being of your aquarium and its inhabitants, making tank care simpler and more effective.

Key Features and Important Notes

  • Three smart modes! Tailor your device to support better health and choose from Plant, Fish, or Shrimp modes.
  • Integrated display and temperature monitoring.
  • Bluetooth & app control. Link your device to the MyChihiros app for remote monitoring and adjustments. You'll also receive alerts when temperatures drift from your set range.
  • Aquarium-safe. Designed to be safe for use with fish, shrimp and aquarium plants. Will not harm beneficial bacteria either so you don't need to worry about crashing cycles or imbalances.
  • Recommended for use in aquariums with TDS levels below 500ppm.

products.body_html:

Driftwood is buoyant and can float upon initial use

Important note: We recommend rinsing wood thoroughly before adding it to an established aquarium. If you prefer, you may also boil the wood for at least 1-2 hours to help release tannins.

products.title: Blistered Driftwood Sticks
products.body_html:
Blistered driftwood sticks are an interesting type of hardscape to use for the planted aquarium and provide a unique look that isn't commonly seen in a standard planted tank. These pieces of natural wood are straight and feature a bumpy texture that would work well when creating forest aquascapes. Alternatively, depending on the tank size, they can be placed at the base for a fallen trunk appearance. Blistered Driftwood Sticks require some creativity and are perfect for anyone who enjoys unusual hardscape in the aquarium.  

Average length: 10-20"

Driftwood can leech tannins which can cause a slight yellow or brown tint to your water.  This is natural and the effects will reduce over time.  Boiling the wood prior to use can decrease the tannins leeching. 

Important note: We recommend rinsing wood thoroughly before adding it to an established aquarium. If you prefer, you may also boil the wood for at least 1-2 hours to help release tannins.

products.body_html:

*We recommend rinsing prior to use

products.title: Anubias Nangi UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Anubias Nangi

Anubias Nangi is a hybrid variant of Anubias created from crossing Anubias Nana and Anubias Gilletii. This Anubias type was first created by Robert A Gasser and has been commonly available to hobbyists since.  

This is an extremely easy plant to grow and can survive a wide range of water parameters. Like other Anubias plants, it can grow in both aquariums and terrariums either submerged or emersed. Anubias is native to the tropical regions of Africa where it can be found growing along streams, rivers and marshes. It's a flowering plant that grows best when the rhizome is attached to a hard surface such as wood or stones.  hey have low to medium light requirements and can benefit from regular fertilization. CO2 is not necessary but can promote faster growth and more robust leaves. Since its a slow grower, the leaves are susceptible to algae growth if placed under high lighting.   

Notes:

  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting. Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly prep tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.

    Family Name: Araceae

    Origin: West Africa

    Height: 6-8”

    pH: 6.0 - 9.0

    Care: Easy

    Light: Low

    Co2: Not necessary

    Propagation: Separate by rhizome

    Growth rate: Slow to moderate

products.body_html:

Bucephalandra Nanga Pinoh features narrow, elongated leaves with subtle ruffled edges and a deep green hue that can take on a bluish sheen under aquarium lighting. Like other Buce varieties, it’s a slow grower that thrives in low to moderate light and doesn’t require CO₂ to do well. This hardy plant can be easily attached to driftwood, stones, or other aquarium decor using glue or thread. Over time, it develops dense clusters of leaves, adding rich texture and depth to any aquascape.

Bucephalandra are known and loved for their color display. To achieve optimal color, CO2 injection, moderate lighting, quality aquarium plant fertilizer and good maintenance habits are highly recommended. New growth can be seen sprouting from the main rhizome. When planting buce, it is important to ensure the rhizome is not buried or suffocated to prevent the plant from dying off.

Notes:

products.body_html:
Common Name: Samolus Parviflorus 'Red'

Samolus parviflorus 'Red' is a hardy and colorful aquatic stem plant that's perfect for your next aquascape! It features soft red and orange hues that are great for adding vibrant pops of color to popular aquascape styles like Dutch-style aquariums and nature style planted tanks. Though a slow grower, it forms full bushes that make it ideal for the midground of your aquarium.

This plant is flexible with care and will do just fine under medium to high lighting. However, providing it with stronger lights will encourage deeper, richer red tones. Under poor lighting or low nutrients, it may revert to thinner, green-toned growth. As with all stem plants, we recommend CO₂ injection and a nutrient-rich substrate for optimal color and rounder, fuller leaves.

Overall, Samolus parviflorus 'Red' is a unique and low-maintenance option for hobbyists looking to add midground red colors.

products.title: Drosera Spatulata 'Labill' Aquatic Farmer Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Round Leaf Sundew

Drosera spatulata is a vibrant and compact carnivorous plant, perfect for both beginners and experienced plant enthusiasts. This sundew species features spoon-shaped leaves covered in tiny, sticky glandular hairs that glisten in the light, attracting and trapping small insects for natural nutrition. This makes Drosera a great addition to any terrarium, or setup with elevated humidity.

Drosera spatulata can develop bold red tones and produces delicate white or pink flowers on slender stalks above the foliage. Its small, tidy rosette shape makes it ideal for grouping with other carnivorous plants or displaying it on its own. So if you're looking for a low-maintenance plant that doesn't require much attention during the colder months, a sundew plant is a great option.

products.title: Selaginella sp. Brown
products.body_html:
Common names: Spikemoss

Selaginella sp. Brown is a fast-growing, moisture-loving fern perfect for vivariums and terrariums. Like most Selaginella, it does not tolerate drying out, so keeping it consistently moist is key to its health and vibrant growth. In a live vivarium or terrarium setup, this species readily climbs upward on backgrounds and decor. It thrives in high humidity environments, but will tolerate slightly lower humidity if misted regularly. Under ideal conditions, it can grow quickly and may require occasional pruning to maintain the desired shape—either climbing or forming a bushy appearance.

This Selaginella prefers low to moderate light, making it easy to incorporate into shaded areas of your tank or terrarium. With proper care, it provides natural texture, and a fast-growing accent to any humid enclosure.

Notes:

  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Selaginella Pulvinata works well in terrarium-like setups and should be partially shaded with good humidity.
  • Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into quality soil. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here.

products.title: Bucephalandra Cascade King
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Cascade King

Bucephalandra Cascade King is a medium-to-large variant of the Bucephalandra genus. The leaves are long and narrow and have slightly ruffled edges. The colors range from green to deep purplish-blue tones. It's highly prized for its pronounced iridescent coloration with appropriate care and when placed under aquarium LED lighting. All Bucephalandra varieties are very slow-growing, with new leaves sprouting from long, thin rhizomes. It can be propagated by cutting the rhizome and attaching it to driftwood and rocks using super glue gel or thread. 

As an epiphytic plant, Bucephalandra generates new growth from its rhizome. It is a slow-grower and can be grown submerged or emersed. Optimal results are achieved for Bucephalandra in low to medium lighting conditions, fostering dense growth when provided with proper water conditions. CO2 injection, adequate water flow, and consistent tank upkeep can result in faster and more robust plant growth. When planting, don't cover the stem and rhizome to prevent plant decay and deterioration. While its care is the same as popular aquatic species like Anubias and Java Ferns, Bucephalandra is less tolerant of abrupt parameter changes.

products.title: Selaginella Pulvinata
products.body_html:
Common names: Spikemoss

Selaginella Pulvinata is an intriguing evergreen perennial that features densely tufted stems and unique curling fronds. Its sems range from 4-15cm long and branch near the base to form rosette-like clusters. Over time, its stems and roots entangle to create a tree-like appearance that's great for planted setups, terrariums and more. Native to limestone rocks, this species is hardy and is sometimes considered drought-resistant, once established, making it a great pick for a range of setups. One of its most fascinating trait is its response to moisture: when dry, young fronds curl tightly, turning yellowish-brown like tiny claws or miniature cypress trees; when moistened, the fronds reopen and regain their vibrant green color.

Notes:

  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Selaginella Pulvinata works well in terrarium-like setups and should be partially shaded with good humidity.
  • Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into quality soil. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here.
  • Yellowing of leaves indicates overwatering.

products.title: Anubias Barteri Broad White UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Anubias Broad White

Anubias Broad White is a farmed variant of the popular Anubias species. This specific variant of Broad Leaf Anubias is extremely rare and highly sought after by enthusiasts for its striking color variation. The leaves are a bit larger than the Anubias Snow White Tissue Culture. Unlike most Anubias species, Broad White requires more light in order to thrive. Their bright coloration makes for a beautiful contrast against darker hardscape material, such as lava rocks, and other dark green aquatic plants.

**Every plant is unique. Leaf color will vary from green to white. Medium to high light & CO2 injecton will bring out the white color. 

Notes:

  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting. Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.
  • These epiphytes can be planted above the substrate or attached to hardscape.
  • Burying the rhizome will cause the plant to melt and die.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly prep tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • These epiphytes can be planted above the substrate or attached to hardscape.
  • Burying the rhizome, will cause the plant to melt and die.

Family Name: Araceae

Origin: West Africa

Height: 3-5”

pH: 6-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Medium-High

Co2: Recommended

Propagation: Separate by rhizome

Growth rate: Slow to moderate

products.title: UNS Reef System - R60 Shallow
products.body_html:
The UNS Reef System offers a reef-ready setup in 6 different sizes. The R line of rimless aquariums features signature UNS 45° mitered edges, black silicone, and thicker, ultra-clear glass ensuring long-term performance. The system includes a matching aluminum-framed stand with a built-in sump filtration system. The stand’s doors are magnetic and fully removable for easy device control and personalized customization. Larger models feature storage drawers for organizing aquarium essentials and more. Each detail of this system was carefully considered and designed to provide the right balance of functionality and aesthetics. 

Rimless Aquarium Features

  • Signature 45° mitered edges and low-iron, high-clarity glass provides an enjoyable viewing experience and enables closer edge-to-edge cleaning with the preferred glass cleaning tool/algae scraper.
  • Black silicone discreetly hides the appearance of coralline
  • This line of aquarium utilizes thicker glass to prevent the need for euro bracing at the top.
  • Tanks feature a sleek white bottom, allowing for a bare-bottom setup without exposing equipment in the sump.

Tank Specifications

  • Dimensions: 23.62 x 19.69 x 11.81" / 60 x 50 x 30CM
  • Glass Thickness: 8mm 
  • Volume: 17.71 gallons / 67L

Stand Specifications

  • Dimensions: 23.62 x 19.69 x 36.2" / 60 x 50 x 92.1CM
  • Door Count: 3
  • Storage Drawers: 0

Sump Specifications

  • Dimensions: 19.69 x 16.85 x 17.72" 
  • Volume: 10.79 gallons / 40.86L
  • Chambers: 4



What's Included

  • UNS R60S Rimless Aquarium
  • Pipe Fittings
  • Union Fittings
  • Outlet Nozzles
  • UNS R60 Stand & Sump (Grey or White)
  • Velcro Strips for wire management

Aquarium Cabinet & Sump Features

  • The overflow box features a low-profile, intake slots at the top and bottom and a vertical piece of glass inside that provides safeguard against flooding of the main tank in the event of pump failure.
  • Overflow lid cover is made from polycarbonate material for increased durability and resistance to heat and warping. Other covers are designed for easy removal and sit flush to reduce salt creep.
  • Fully removable, magnetic doors. Aluminum frame and magnetic hold remove the need for traditional stainless steel door hinges, improving resistance to rust.
  • Comes fully assembled with sump pre-installed for easy setup and straightforward plumbing. Simply place the tank on top, connect the pre-glued pipes and secure each connection with easy twist-on unions.
  • Pre-cut holes for wire management.
  • All models, excluding the R60, include pull-out drawers for extra storage and organization of accessories, tools and more.
  • Built-in controller box provides a dedicated space for surge protector and optimal cable management. Bottom section may be used for a chiller and includes a viewing window for easy monitoring. (R120 and larger)
  • Controller boards are removable and can be easily accessed by removing the side door while ensuring wires remain tucked away.


Sump Filtration System Features

  • Gate valves are included for smooth water flow and reduced noise. Additional ball valve and barbed fittings are included on the return line in the event external equipment such as reactors or chillers are being used. Included gasket below the drain pipe and sump to reduce salt creep accumulation.
  • The multi-chamber sump features a prefilter baffle to trap larger solids on filter floss, reducing the frequency of filter sock replacement. Diffuser plates are included to reduce noise and prevent floating of filter socks when replacement/maintenance is near.
  • Second chamber will accommodate a variety of protein skimmers on the market. Water levels can be adjusted via the acrylic baffle to accommodate various height requirements of different protein skimmers.
  • Third chamber may be used for additional desired media or as a refugium. Water levels may be adjusted as desired.
  • Includes acrylic ATO with preinstalled float valve for automatic top-offs.

products.title: Davallia Parvula
products.body_html:
Common Name: Davallia Parvula

Davallia parvula is a small fern prized for its delicate, finely textured foliage and distinctive fuzzy rhizomes. This compact species is especially well-suited for terrariums, paludariums, or vivariums, where it can thrive in moderate to high humidity and medium to bright indirect light. Like other Davallia ferns, it grows from a creeping rhizome covered in fine hairs that slowly spreads over hardscape or substrate, adding a natural feel to its surroundings. Over time, it forms a lush mat of fronds that results in a soft moss-like texture.

For best results, mount this fern where its rhizome remains consistently moist but its foliage remains dry between mistings to prevent rot.

Key Features & Important Notes:

  • This plant is shipped bare root. Each plant will have 2-4 leaves per order.
  • This is not an aquatic plant and is best suited for terrariums or paludariums.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

products.title: UNS Reef System - R180 Peninsula Limited Edition
products.body_html:
The UNS Reef System peninsula line offers a reef-ready setup in three different sizes. The R line of rimless aquariums features signature UNS 45° mitered edges, black silicone and thicker, ultra-clear glass ensuring long term performance. The system includes a matching aluminum-framed stand with a built-in sump filtration system. The stand’s doors are magnetic and fully removable for easy device control and personalized customization. The peninsula line features doors on the front, left, and back side. Models include storage drawers for organization of aquarium essentials and more. Each detail of this system was carefully considered and designed to provide the right balance of functionality and aesthetic. Learn more about the details below.


Key Features

  • 1 year warranty

Rimless Aquarium Features

  • Clear silicone, high-end finishes
  • All white, ultra-slim glass overflow to reduce intrusion on main display aquarium.
  • Signature 45° mitered edges and low-iron, high-clarity glass provides an enjoyable viewing experience and enables closer edge to edge cleaning with preferred glass cleaning tool / algae scraper.
  • This line of aquarium utilizes thicker glass to prevent the need for euro bracing at the top.
  • Tanks feature a sleek white bottom, allowing for a bare-bottom setup without exposing equipment in the sump.

Aquarium Cabinet & Sump Features

  • The peninsula overflow box features a thin-profile, intake slots at the top and bottom and a vertical piece of glass inside that provides safeguard against flooding of the main tank in the event of pump failure.
  • Overflow lid cover is made from polycarbonate material for increased durability and resistance to heat and warping. Other covers are designed for easy removal and sit flush to reduce salt creep.
  • Fully removable, magnetic doors. Aluminum frame and magnetic hold remove the need for traditional stainless steel door hinges, improving resistance to rust.
  • Comes fully assembled with sump pre-installed for easy setup and straightforward plumbing. Simply place the tank on top, connect the pre-glued pipes and secure each connection with easy twist-on unions.
  • All models, excluding the R60, include pull-out drawers for extra storage and organization of accessories, tools and more.
  • Pre-cut holes for wire management above the pull-out drawer. 
  • Built-in controller box provides a dedicated space for surge protector and optimal cable management. Bottom section may be used for a chiller and includes a viewing window for easy monitoring. (R90 and larger)
  • Controller boards are removable and can be easily accessed by removing the side door while ensuring wires remain tucked away.
  • Three drilled holes on the horizontal bar above the pull out drawer allows users to run wavemaker wires cleanly into the electrical cabinet.
  • Cable management boxes inside of the stand to hide surge protector wires in.

Sump Filtration System Features

  • Frosted blue glass sump with matching white plumbing.
  • Gate valves are included for smooth water flow and reduced noise. Additional ball valve and barbed fittings are included on the return line in the event external equipment such as reactors or chillers are being used. Included gasket below the drain pipe and sump to reduce salt creep accumulation.
  • The multi-chamber sump features a prefilter baffle to trap larger solids on filter floss, reducing the frequency of filter sock replacement. Diffuser plates are included to reduce noise and prevent floating of filter socks when replacement/maintenance is near.
  • Second chamber will accommodate a variety of protein skimmers on the market. Water levels can be adjusted via the acrylic baffle to accommodate various height requirements of different protein skimmers.
  • Third chamber may be used for additional desired media or as a refugium. Water levels may be adjusted as desired.
  • Includes acrylic ATO with preinstalled float valve for automatic top-offs.

  • Door Count: 9
  • Storage Drawers: 1

Sump Specifications

  • Dimensions: 43.31 x 20.79 x 17.72" / 110 x 52.8 x 45CM
  • Volume: 37 gallons / 141L
  • Total Chambers: 4

  • UNS R180 Peninsula Rimless Aquarium
  • Pipe Fittings

products.title: Cryptocoryne Mioya on Driftwood
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne grown on Driftwood

Cryptocoryne is a commonly found aquatic plant in planted aquariums. Generally hardy, Cryptocoryne species will do well in a wide range of aquarium conditions and is a great choice for beginner hobbyists. This particular Cryptocoryne comes readily attached to a piece of aquarium driftwood and can be simply placed into a planted aquarium for an instant aquascape style. The type of Cryptocoryne received may vary and photos are a representation of what will be received.

Cryptocoryne species grow at a slow to moderate rate depending on the environment in which they're kept. There is a wide variety of species in the planted tank hobby and are a great addition to the midground of planted aquariums. Once placed, Cryptocoryne does not typically like to be moved so care should be taken to plan out its placement first! Cryptocoryne can be grown submerged or emersed but ample amounts of humidity should be provided if grown above water. Propagation is simple and can be done by separating new plantlets growing and replanting or reattaching.  

Notes:
  • Cryptocoryne prefer not to be moved once planted. If possible, do not move them!
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • This product is aquarium ready and can be placed into your tank instantly.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

 

Family Name: Araceae
Endemic To: Borneo
Leaves: varies
pH: 6-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
Co2: Not necessary but recommended
Propagation: Cut by rhizome
Growth rate: Slow to Moderate

products.title: Hydrocleys Nymphoides Water Poppy
products.body_html:
Common Name: Water Poppy

Hydrocleys nymphoides is an aquatic plant known for its glossy green leaves and bright yellow, poppy-like flowers. Found in shallow waters, it's a beautiful and functional choice for ponds or paludariums that have an emersed section. Water poppies add texture and simultaneously help filter water, ensuring your setup is clean and healthy. This plant is generally easy to care for, but needs strong lighting. In outdoor settings, it prefers full sun exposure for a minimum of 6 hours. If you plan on growing it indoors, we recommend placing it in an area that receives plenty of light to support growth. Once it takes off, trim it regularly to manage its spread. It can be propagated easily by trimming along its runners and replanting.

Notes:

  • While Water Poppies can float, they grow best when planted in nutritious aquarium soil. Plant them in shallow areas of your setup and if needed, add extra nutrients with root tabs or an aquatic plant fertilizer.
  • Not frost-hardy. In colder climates, keep your plant indoors and provide good lighting.
  • Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Alismataceae
Care: Easy
Light: High
Propagation: Trim and replant runners
Growth rate: Moderate to fast

products.title: Java Fern Windelov 'Hybrid'
products.body_html:
Common Name: Java Fern Windelov, Microsorum Pteropus

Java Fern Windelov 'Hybrid' is a unique cultivar that combines traits from both classic Windelov and Coral. This aquarium fern features fatter leaves with split tips that branch outwards. The splitting appears to be randomized, resulting in an organic look that's perfect for aquascaping and planted tanks. Alternatively, its bushy foliage would work great in terrarium-like setups where humidity is elevated!

Like other Java Fern plants, Windelov 'Hybrid' is a hardy, beginner-friendly choice. It thrives in low to medium light and is adaptable to a wide range of water parameters. It grows best when attached directly to driftwood, rocks or any aquarium decor and would make a great addition to any low tech planted tank. To avoid damaging the plant, keep the rhizome above substrate and place it in a spot with good flow. Propagation is easy and straightforward; when the plant is ready, simply cut or pull apart rhizomes to be attached as desired.

  • Click HERE for a Java Fern Care Guide

Notes:

  • Java Ferns grow best when attached to aquascaping hardscape like driftwood and stones. These plants propagate through its leaves. Dots on the underside of its leaves are normal and are not a cause for concern.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Remove the cotton surrounding the roots before planting. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here.
  • Burying the rhizome will cause the plant to die
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Polypodiaceae
Height: 3-5”
pH: 6-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low
Co2: Not Required
Propagation: Separate by Rhizome
Growth rate: Slow to Moderate

products.title: Echinodorus Ozelot Narrow Mini
products.body_html:
Common Name: Ozelot Narrow Mini 

Echinodorus Ozelot Narrow Mini are very popular in the aquarium hobby, being that they are beautiful and easy to care for. They offer excellent and attractive coverage to the background of an aquascape. These plants have short stems that grow in a rosette, bearing long and ovoid leaves with a pointed tip. 

These plants are a great fit for a large tank. Their large leaves can easily restrict light to other plants if they are not pruned regularly. These plants propagate through side shoots off of their leaves, which can be trimmed and replanted into the substrate. To control the size of the plant, you can remove the side shoots and older leaves.

With a nutrient-rich substrate, these plants will tolerate most lighting conditions, but moderate to high lighting is ideal. CO2 injection is not necessary but will help the plant grow faster if desired. Amazon sword plants will grow submersed or emersed. Emersed plants produce

products.title: Bucephalandra Pearl Grey
products.body_html:
Buce Pearl Grey is an extremely rare variant in the Bucephalandra family so there is little to no information known about this aquatic plant. With looks similar to Buce Skeleton King, Buce Pearl Grey also features rich dark green tones on large leaves. Unlike Buce Dark Skeleton King, Buce Dark Grey has smooth leaves and does not have a ribbed texture from its veins. Despite little information being available, Buce Pearl Grey is similar in care and requirements to all other Bucephalandra species. It is very hardy and does exceptionally well in a range of aquarium conditions making it a great choice for all aquarium hobbyists. Buce Pearl Grey will do best in low to medium lighting along with CO2 if possible. Being part of the Buce family, it does grow at a slow pace and can be kept submerged in an aquarium or immersed in similar setups. Buce Pearl Grey can be propagated by cutting the rhizome and reattaching new portions to aquarium driftwood or stones with super glue or thread. This species of Bucephalandra is definitely a must-have for all Buce lovers!

Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant so it can be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not plant Bucephalandra in substrate and cover the rhizome. This will cause it to melt and rot.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the rockwool surrounding the roots and attach to hardscape. For instructions on how to properly prep "potted" aquarium plants, click here.

products.title: Dymax Feeding Tray
products.body_html:
The Dymax Feeding Tray is a sleek method to keep food from flowing away and possibly overflowing into the filtration system. Feeding trays float on the surface, creating a barrier to trap food. Floating fish food, such as pellets and flake, will be kept within the tray for controlled food dispersal.

In time, fish and invertebrates will recognize where to expect food, leading to a stronger feeding response and less wasted food. The Dymax Feeding Tray is suitable for both freshwater and saltwater aquariums, and it is available in two different sizes.

products.body_html:
The Dymax CO2 Drop Checker is a great and essential option to monitor the CO2 levels in an aquarium. It is difficult to know how much CO2 is dissolved in the water and too little or too much can have devastating effects on the aquarium. Going off the number of bubbles in a bubble counter is not always a fool proof method to monitor CO2 concentrations in an aquarium. Too little CO2 may promote algae growth and poor plant growth, while too much CO2 will be detrimental to livestock. With the Dymax CO2 Drop Checker, aquarist can easily know when the correct concentration of CO2 has been achieved in the aquarium.

Set Up:

  • Start by filling the drop checker half way with the CO2 Indicator Solution.
  • Attach the suction cup to the drop checker 
  • Gently place in the tank, being careful not the spill the indicator solution

How to Read:

  • Blue- CO2 concentration is too low
  • Green- CO2 concentration is good
  • Yellow- CO2 concentration is too high
  • Be aware that the drop checker takes 1-2 hours to react to the concentration of CO2 in the aquarium. 

Includes:

  • High Quality Drop Checker
  • Suction cup

Dimensions:

Large 2.5 x 1.2"

 

**Kits include indicator solution and drop checker**

products.body_html:
Anacharis is one of the easiest aquatic plants to keep and highly suitable for beginners thanks to its lack of care requirements. It can be kept free floating in the aquarium or anchored into the substrate depending on preference. Although it prefers cooler water temperatures, it can survive in warm water as well. If this is your first time trying live plants, Anacharis is highly recommended as it is pretty hard to kill this plant.  

Its leaves are thinner with slight texture when compared to its popular alternative Egeria Densa. Give it a try if you're looking to add some good color and easy plants to your freshwater planted aquarium.

*Please be aware that some aquatic plants may be classified as restricted or noxious weeds in certain states. It is the responsibility of the customer to stay informed and up to date with their local and state regulations before purchasing.

Family Name: Hydrocharitaceae
Origin: South America
Height: 4-12”
pH: 6.5-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low to medium
Co2: Not required
Propagation: Cut stem and replant

products.title: Saxifraga Stolonifera (Strawberry Begonia)
products.body_html:
Common Name: Strawberry Begonia

Strawberry Begonia (Saxifraga stolonifera) is a terrestrial plant with rounded, fuzzy leaves accented by silver veining and soft red undersides. This species is not aquatic and should not be kept submerged in aquarium conditions. Instead, it thrives in terrariums or the emersed sections of a paludarium! The plant spreads via delicate runners, much like a spider plant, and will create a cascading effect over time.

Native to Asia, Strawberry Begonia is also popular as a hanging plant. It can tolerate a range of humidity levels, including average household levels, making it great for beginner plant lovers. Care is straightforward! Be sure to avoid letting water sit on its leaves and fertilize it every two weeks or once a month. Lighting needs are flexible: it can grow in moderate lighting, but appreciates bright spots as long as its partially shaded or filtered. When kept as a houseplant, allow the soil to dry slightly between waterings and use a potting mix with aerating ingredients like perlite. Eventually it will grow off-shoots with baby plants attached that you can propagate for more mother plants!

Notes: 

  • Strawberry Begonia is a terrestrial plant, and will not survive if submerged in aquariums.
  • May be used in terrariums, but avoid placing it in an area that is too moist.
  •  Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Saxifragaceae
Care: Easy
Light: Shaded Light to bright indirect light
Water: Allow soil to dry between watering
Propagation: Separate stolons / plantlets
Growth rate: Medium

products.title: Bacopa Monnieri Platinum
products.body_html:
Common Name: Water Hyssop, Moneywort

Moneywort is a popular back or mid-ground stem plant. This variegated variety features the same pairs of leaves sprouting from the base to the tip of the stalk in a mixture of green and cream tones. With the classic being an aquarium hobby staple, both cultivars add lovely accent to any planted tank.

This plant can be great for beginners because it is hardy and adapts well to most aquarium conditions. It does prefer a nutrient-rich environment and clean water. It requires moderate light or the lower parts of the stems. Moneywort is propagated through cuttings that easily take root in the substrate. It can be grown submersed or emersed and will bloom tiny pink and white flowers above water.

Notes:

  • Bacopa is extremely hardy aquarium plants and is a great beginner choice. However, its leaves are fragile and should be handled with care. 
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its lead bunch. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly plant "lead bunch" aquarium plants, click here.

Family Name: Plantaginaceae
Origin: Widespread
Height: 4-12”
pH: 6.0-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Moderate to High
Co2: Not necessary
Propagation: Cuttings
Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Bucephalandra Mini Dark Needle
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Mini Dark Needle

Bucephalandra Mini Dark Needle is a smaller Bucephalandra variation that features small, dark green leaves with reddish sheaths. Like others, this Bucephalandra is very slow-growing and sprouts new leaves from its central rhizome. It can be propagated by separating the rhizome gently and attaching it to driftwood and rocks using super glue gel or thread. 

It thrives under low to moderate lighting and adapts well to various water parameters. Bucephalandra can be grown submerged or emersed. Like Anubias, Buce plants have rhizomes that attach and grow on solid surfaces. While it attaches exceptionally well to rocks, it can also flourish on driftwood and other substrates. When placed in the substrate, ensure only a portion of the Buce plant is under the substrate while keeping the rhizome exposed, as burying it can lead to rot.

Key Features & Important Notes

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant so it can be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not plant Bucephalandra in substrate and cover the rhizome. This will cause it to melt and rot.
  • Bucephalandra’s final size, coloration and growth rate are dependent upon the condition of the aquarium Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.

    Care & Additional Information 
    Listed information should be treated as general guidelines only. We encourage you to do thorough research before keeping any type of livestock.

    Family Name: Araceae
    Origin: Borneo
    pH: 6-8
    Skill Level: Easy
    Light: Low to Medium
    Co2: Not necessary but recommended 
    Propagation: Separate by rhizome
    Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost

Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants.  They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.  

Buce Brownie Ghost is a beautiful variant in the Bucephalandra family group. This aquatic plant is, like almost all Buce, very hardy and can be grown submerged in an aquarium or emerged in a terrarium or paludarium. Like other bucephalandra species, Brownie Ghost doesn't require too much light and adapts quickly to a wide range of water parameters. Its care is similar to that of Cryptocoryne and Anubias species. It features beautiful coloration that can vary depending on its environment and will easily attach itself to aquarium hardscape material such as stones or aquarium driftwood. If given optimal care, Buce Brownie Ghost can grow to be very beautiful and will also provide a safe haven for smaller aquatic life such as shrimp, fish, and fry!

Like all Buce, this aquatic plant grows extremely slow and it can take almost half a year for this tissue culture cup to grow in. It can be propagated by cutting the rhizome and attached to driftwood and rocks using super glue gel or thread. 

Important Notes:

  • Bucephalandra is an epiphytic aquarium plant so it can be attached to aquarium hardscape.
  • Do not plant Bucephalandra in substrate and cover the rhizome. This will cause it to melt and rot.
  • Bucephalandra’s final size, coloration and growth rate are dependent upon the condition of the aquarium Photos are a representation of what you will receive and may vary.
  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting.  Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.
