products.title: Anubias Nana Peacock
products.body_html:
Common Name: Anubias Nana Peacock

Anubias Nana 'Peacock' is a variety of Anubias Barteri var. nana that's popular within the aquascaping community. These plants are easy to care for and great for beginners and experienced aquarium hobbyists alike. Anubias is a classic rhizome plant that sprouts new growth from its horizontal stem. Like other forms, it grows well when attached to porous surfaces like driftwood or aquarium stones. This quality makes it versatile with placement in tanks! It's also capable of growing in emersed conditions, making it suitable for paludariums, vivariums and terrariums. 

Like other forms, Nana Peacock is very similar to regular Nana and features deep green color and broad leaves that are great for filling in empty space. It's a slow grower and can be susceptible to algae growth if placed under lighting that's too strong. Make sure to provide good flow, CO2 (if possible) and a quality plant fertilizer for best results.

Anubias Specifications
Stats provided should be viewed as general guidelines. Factors such as height, size and overall success can vary depending on care and tank conditions.

  • Family name: Araceae
  • Origin: Africa
  • Care: Easy
  • Lighting: Low to medium
  • CO2: Not required, but recommended
  • Propagation: Separate at rhizome
  • Growth rate: Slow 

Notes:

  • Anubias are strong plants that can thrive in a variety of environments.
  • Anubias are epiphytic. These hardy epiphytes can be planted above the substrate or attached to hardscape.
  • Burying the rhizome, will cause the plant to melt and die.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. For instructions on how to properly prep aquarium plants, click here. 
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt of the aquarium plant.

products.title: Aquario Neo Fluid No CO2 - Liquid Fertilizer
products.body_html:
Aquario Neo Fluid No CO2 is a complete liquid fertilizer designed for planted aquariums without CO2 injection. The formula contains nitrogen, phosphate, potassium, iron, and trace elements—so no additional fertilizers are needed. With regular use and good aquarium care habits, this fertilizer supports faster plant growth, stronger root systems, and the development of fresh new leaves! Great for tanks that contain stem plants or species that require good nutrition levels for proper growth. 

Dosage & Directions

  • 1 capful (on 300ml bottles) = 5ml
  • Shake well before use.
  • Dose 2 caps (10ml) per 10 gallons (40L) every 3 days.
  • Reduce dose by half following major trims.

Important Notes

  • For aquarium use only.
  • May settle, separate or have a slight smell. This is normal and does not indicate defective or expired product.
  • Keep out of reach of children and pets.
  • Do not use with other liquid fertilizers.

products.title: CO2 Perma-Seal O-Ring
products.body_html:
Perma-Seal is used to prevent leaks between the CO2 cylinder tank and the CO2 regulator. Using a Perma-Seal replaces the need for cheap plastic O-rings that are commonly used when installing CO2 Regulators onto the gas tank. Plastic o-rings need to be replaced periodically because the gas pressure distorts the plastic over time. If they aren't replaced, this has the potential to lead to CO2 leaks and frequent refills to provide a consistent flow of CO2 to a planted aquarium. There is no need for replacement with the Perma-seal. Simply screw it into the CGA-320 connection of your gas tank by using an Allen key before installing your CO2 regulator.  

This product works perfectly with the UNS Regulators

products.title: Seiryu Accent Stones
products.body_html:
Seiryu Accent Stones contain the smallest whole pieces of Boutique Seiryu. Accent stones are crucial for creating a realistic hardscape layout by providing depth and detail to an aquascape. Smaller stones placed at the base of larger centerpieces add a sense of realism. These smaller stones are all you need for aquascaping a nano or pico tank.  

Seiryu is the Godfather of all stones. The pinnacle of aquascaping rock. Undoubtedly one of the most popular hardscape materials available. The craggy texture and endless details in these stones allow anyone to easily create a natural looking aquascape.  

Seiryu may slightly raise pH and water hardness. Controsoil can help balance that out. 

Pieces from packages will range from 1-3 inches. Each bag is approximately 3LB per order.

products.title: Dragon Stone (Ohko) Accent Stones
products.body_html:
Dragon Stone, also known as Ohko Stone, is a popular aquascaping rock named after it's scale-like texture. The natural details and crevices give your aquascape an aged look that can't be duplicated with artificial decorations. Dragon stone can come with a wide array of colors, ranging for reds, yellows, and browns. Its clay-like composition makes it brittle compared to other rocks, allowing aquatic plants to easily self-attach.

Dragon Stone is inert and will not influence your water chemistry, making it the ideal layout material for any aquarium. 

Pieces from packages will range from 1-3", making them a perfect choice for nano aquarium scapes or adding finer details to an existing scape. Each bag is approximately 2.2LB per order.

Stones will require rinsing prior to use to remove any dust and excess clay.

products.title: Hedyotis Salzmannii
products.body_html:
Common Name: Hedyotis Salzmannii

Hedyotis Salzmannii is another aquarium plant with very little information available. Hailing from South America, Hedyotis Salzmannii is very similar in appearance to Bacopa Monnieri. It features bright green stalks with green leaves sprouting from it. From what is known, it is generally an easy aquatic plant and will readily grow in most aquarium conditions. Just like Bacopa, it is propagated through cuttings that easily take root in the substrate. It can be grown submersed or emersed. 

Notes: 

  • Hedyotis Salzmannii is similar to Bacopa in appearance and generally hardy in nature. Provide it with good lighting and quality soil at minimum.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly plant "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Plantaginaceae

Origin: Widespread

Height: 4-12”

pH: 6.0-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Moderate to High

Co2: Not necessary

Propagation: Cuttings

Growth rate: Fast

products.title: Rorippa Aquatica
products.body_html:
Common Name: Water Cabbage, Armoracia Aquatica

Water Cabbage is an undemanding plant that prefers lower temperatures with moderate lighting and a nutrient-rich substrate.  It is a small, rosette plant with interesting leaves which can vary in shape and size depending on growing conditions.  

Like many other stem plants, this plant can grow both emersed and submerged.  It is a fast grower and can require frequent trimming when kept under high light with Co2 and the addition of fertilizers.  Like most stem plants, propagation can be done simply by cutting along the stem and replanting the cuttings.

Family: Brassicaceae

Region: North America

Height: 2-6"

pH: 6-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Moderate to High

Co2: Not required

Propagation: Cut stem and replant

Growth rate: Fast

products.title: Rotala Florida Sunset Aquatic Farmer Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Rotalia ramosior Florida, Rotala Florida

Rotala Florida Sunset, is as its name implies, a plant most easily found in Florida, USA. Considered to be a rare stem plant, Rotala Florida Sunset is highly coveted and is not the easiest species to propagate successfully. Its most distinctive and attractive feature is the plant's captivating purple leaves. High light and CO2 injection, additionally regular fertilization are all required for this plants beauty to reach its fullest potential. Rotala Florida Sunset grows slower when compared to other species and will require time and patience to achieve good coverage.

We recommend placing Rotala Florida Sunset as a background or midground plant. Its vivid purple coloration will be an attention grabber and bring depth to your aquascape. Rotala Florida Sunset can be grown in both submerged and emersed conditions, although a transition period will be required. To keep Rotala Florida Sunset nice and compact, regular trimming will need to be performed.

    Notes:

    • You may receive this plant in its emersed or submerged form depending on availability and seasonality. Click to learn more about Emersed vs. Submerged-Grown Aquatic Plants for Aquascaping.
    • Rotala has fragile leaves! Be sure to handle with care. 
    • Intensity of coloration, final size, and growth rate is largely based on the conditions this plant is kept in. This is a live plant meaning size, shape and color may vary.
    • Please be sure to remove this plant from its wrap. Remove the foam surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep “bunched“ aquarium plants, click here.
    • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt of the aquarium plant.
    • High CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil/sufficient nutrients will yield better growth.
    • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

    Rotala Florida Sunset Specifications
    Please note that all stats are provided as general guidelines. Factors such as height, size and overall success can vary depending on care and environment.

    • Family name: Lythraceae
    • Origin: America
    • Height: 7-15"
    • Care: Medium
    • Lighting: Medium to High
    • CO2: Required
    • Propagation: Cut stem and replant or lateral shoots
    • Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Ludwigia Inclinata var. Verticillata 'Pantanal' Aquatic Farmer Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Ludwigia Inclinata ' Pantanal'

Ludwigia inclinata var. verticillata 'Pantanal' is an orange-red variety of Ludwigia inclinata. It’s a fast-growing stem plant with delicate reddish leaves radiating from a central stem. Native to South America, it’s been part of the aquarium hobby for years, but due to its high care requirements, it’s less popular than other easier-to-grow colored plants. Ludwigia 'Pantanal' is best suited as a centerpiece or background plant, but it grows rapidly when kept in ideal conditions. This fast growth can make it tricky to maintain in aquascapes, as the size and shape of the plant will constantly change with regular pruning.

This plant requires high light, soft water, nutrients and CO2 injection to grow well. It is a finicky plant and will quickly show signs when conditions are not optimal or changed. The plant can be grown submersed or emersed. If successful, propagation can be achieved by taking cuttings from the main stem of the plant that are replanted into the substrate.

Notes:

  • Ludwigia can be grown both submerged or emersed. You may receive this plant in its emersed or submerged form depending on availability and seasonality. Click to learn more about Emersed vs. Submerged-Grown Aquatic Plants for Aquascaping.
  • Ludwigia can be very fragile. Be sure to handle this aquarium plant with care.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Onagraceae
Origin: South America
Height: 6-20”
Care: Advanced
Light: High
Co2: Recommended
Propagation: Cuttings
Growth Rate: Fast

products.title: Eleocharis Parvula 'Mini' Aquatic Farmer Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Dwarf Hair Grass, Eleocharis Parvulus Mini

Dwarf Hair Grass is an extremely popular and easy to keep foreground aquarium plant that produces a grass-like lawn when provided with optimal conditions. Known for being an aquatic grass, Dwarf Hair Grass features thin, bright green blades that spread horizontally by sending out runners.  

High-lighting and Co2 injection are not mandatory to grow dwarf hair grass but implementing this in a planted tank help to promote and increase the growth rate and density of the aquarium plant. Dwarf Hair Grass is a versatile aquarium plant that can also grow submerged or emersed in dry-start aquariums and Wabi Kusa setups. When grown in optimal conditions, Dwarf Hair Grass will grow quickly and frequent pruning will be needed to ensure the foreground stays well-trimmed and low. Dwarf Hair Grass will also willingly travel to all areas of a planted aquarium if given the opportunity!

Need Dwarf Hair Grass but this listing is out of stock? Check out these other variants:

  • Dwarf Hair Grass - Eleocharis Belem
  • Dwarf Hair Grass - Eleocharis Pusillis

Notes:

  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting.  Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection will yield better growth.

Family Name: Cyperaceae

Origin: Cosmopolitan

Height: 1-2”

pH: 6-7.5

Care: Easy

Light: Medium to High

Co2: Recommended 

Propagation: Cut new growth along with roots

Growth rate: Moderate to Fast

products.title: Alternanthera Reineckii Rosanervig Aquatic Farmer Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Alternanthera Reineckii Rosanervig features vivid pink leaves with lightly colored veins. Similar to other species in the Alternanthera family, this aquatic plant will grow best when provided with good aquarium lighting and consistent fertilization. This species is slightly more sensitive and has a softer stem, so care should be taken when handling this aquatic plant. If given the best care, this aquatic plant is striking as a background choice in smaller aquariums or as a midground in larger planted tanks. 

Notes:

  • Alternanthera provide great coverage for sensitive species and are relatively easier choices for beginners who are looking for colored aquarium plants.
  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting.  Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.  
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • For instructions on how to properly plant tissue culture aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.

Origin: South America
pH: 6.0-7.0
Care: Easy
Light: Medium to High
Co2: Recommended
Propagation: Cut stem and replant
Growth rate: Moderate to Fast

products.title: Persicaria Hydropiper
products.body_html:
Common Name: Persicaria Hydropiper, Polygonum sp.

Persicaria hydropiper is a unique, fast-growing stem like plant that can be added to emersed sections of your aquascape. It produces vivid green and red colors that work great in paludariums given its fast, marginal growth in natural settings. It can adapt to submerged conditions where its leaves become softer and may produce green and pink tones. Persicaria is a good option for aquarium hobbyists or plant enthusiasts in search of a unique plant that can be grown many different ways. It's a fast grower and will need to be pruned on occasion!

Like many other stem plants, providing medium to high light with the addition of CO2 will improve health and growth. This plant may come in bunched or potted form depending on availability.

Notes: 

  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its wrap. Remove the plant weight and foam surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep “bunched“ aquarium plants, click here. 
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

products.title: Nano Horn Wood
products.body_html:
Horn Wood is a dense natural driftwood with varying textures and plenty of character, similar to other popular choices like Spider Wood and Pacific Driftwood! Its warm color is versatile and can be used in planted aquariums, terrariums or hybrid paludariums.

Many pieces feature natural branching along with shapes that are great for base pieces, making it easy to combine several to create unique hardscape layouts alone or with your favorite stones. Its textured surface and colors add depth and are great for attaching mosses or aquatic epiphytes like Anubias and Bucephalandra. 

Size Specifications
Photos provided are a representation of what you will receive (some are enlarged to depict texture). Horn Wood is a natural product so coloration, shape and size differences will occur and are to be expected. 

  • Nano: 4-10" (average)
  • Available in 5 and 10 packs. We recommend a 5 pack for smaller aquariums up to 10 gallons. For larger tanks, 10 packs will provide more variation in size and shape for your scape.

Notes:

  • Rinse thoroughly before use. Like most natural driftwood, it will release tannins into the water at first. This effect is temporary and will reduce over time.
  • Aquarium driftwood is buoyant and will float. Please ensure your piece is fully waterlogged prior to use in a planted tank or it will float upon filling the aquarium.
  • If you cannot boil or soak prior to use, try placing stones or heavy decor on top to ensure your pieces stay submerged.
  • Natural white film may appear during the beginning stages. This is natural and can be siphoned out during routine maintenance.

products.title: Eriocaulon sp Vietnam
products.body_html:
Common Name: Vietnamese Eriocaulon

The Vietnamese Eriocaulon is a southeast Asian plant that grows wildly in stagnant as well as flowing waters. It grows with a tall stem, and features long, whitish, needlelike leaves. The plant also grows many lateral shoots, which enhance its bushy look.

While beautiful, this plant is not easy to grow. It needs an acidic substrate, soft water, and a high amount of light and CO2 injection. It needs a balanced nutrient content, including nitrate, phosphate, iron, and micronutrients. In fact, due to these tough requirements, some hobbyists grow the species in its own tank.

For propagation, one must cut the plant carefully and not often: Vietnamese Eriocaulon does not respond well to trimming. It’s typically best to simply replace the trimmed plant with the new ones, and the cuttings should not exceed 2” in length.

Overall, it is a beautiful plant, perfect as a green accent plant or filler in the midground or background, and its growth rate and leaves make it ideal for Dutch style tanks.

Genus: Eriocaulon

Family Name: Eriocaulaceae

Origin: Vietnam

Height:

pH: 4.5 - 7.0

Care: Difficult

Light: High

Co2: High

Propagation: Cuttings

Growth Rate: Medium

products.title: Mopani Wood
products.body_html:
Mopani wood is a popular natural material commonly used for freshwater aquariums and other related hobbies. It is an African hardwood that unlike other popular driftwoods, is extremely dense and resistant to rot. This is a great option for aquarium hobbyists who are looking for a wood option that's much easier to sink and won't break down over time. However, like all natural driftwood, Mopani will release tannins and produce that tea-colored tint that can be reduced with consistent aquarium maintenance. 

Aside from planted tanks, Mopani is often used for terrariums and reptile tanks as well. It features a two-toned, smooth surface with gnarled textured throughout for added character and visual interest. 

Size Specifications

products.title: White Galaxy Long Fin Medaka Rice Fish
products.body_html:
**Note: All livestock orders will come carefully packaged with solid wall Styrofoam insulation and shipped via 1-day shipping only. No extra insulation needs to be added to your cart for your livestock.

Medaka ricefish are a hardy and adaptable freshwater species that works well in community aquariums, ponds and planted tanks!

Medaka are popular in pond-style tanks and provide a unique top-down view for the hobbyist to enjoy. Like other species of Ricefish, they are very hardy and adaptable to a wide variety of aquarium parameters and salinity levels, making them a solid beginner aquarium fish. 

Ricefish are peaceful and should be kept in aquariums with other peaceful fish and larger peaceful invertebrates. Ricefish are social and should be kept in schools of 6 or more for good health and livelihood. Ricefish do have the potential to jump, so it is recommended that coverage be available if a well-fitting lid is not being used. Like most species of aquarium fauna, the White Galaxy Medaka Ricefish will appreciate the addition of live aquarium plants to the tank as well.

Ricefish are omnivorous and will eat a wide variety of fish food. High-quality flakes, pellets, live or frozen will be readily accepted by Ricefish. 

Medaka Ricefish Care
Click here for a complete Japanese Rice Fish Care Guide

  • Temperament: Peaceful - Should be kept with peaceful species
  • Average Size: 0.5-1"
  • Diet: Omnivore
  • Tank Size: 10G minimum is highly recommended for a school

products.title: Myriophyllum Elatinoides 'Snowflake' UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Myriophyllum Elatinoides 'Snowflake', Watermilfoil

Myriophyllum elatinoides, commonly known as Water Milfoil, is a beautiful stem plant known for its delicate, needle-like foliage and tall growth habits. Its vibrant, bright green color and fine-textured leaves make it an excellent choice for adding contrast, texture, and visual movement to the background of a planted aquarium. This species grows well in medium to high lighting conditions—typically around 2 to 4 watts per gallon. Under optimal lighting, Myriophyllum elatinoides exhibits rapid growth and produces dense, symmetrical whorls of foliage that resemble the branches of a pine or fir tree. In lower light, however, growth slows noticeably, and the iconic feathery leaves may appear more sparse.

Versatile in its placement, this plant can be planted into the substrate or left to float freely. When planted in small, spaced-out clusters, it creates a lush, forest-like effect, especially when contrasted against broader-leaved or darker-toned plants. With adequate lighting, spacing, and basic nutrient support, Myriophyllum can quickly become a soft, centerpiece or a dynamic backdrop that elevates the depth of your tank's layout.

Notes:

  • Pruning will be required to ensure other plants are not shaded from lighting.
  • Myriophyllum species makes a great midground or background filler plant choice!
  • Relatively hardy and adaptable to a range of aquarium parameters. 
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Genus: South America
Family Name: Haloragaceae
Origin: Australia
Height: 20” - 30”
pH: 5.0 - 7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Medium to High
Co2: Medium to High
Propagation: Cutting
Growth Rate: Fast

products.title: Myriophyllum Aquaticum 'Roraima' UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Myriophyllum sp. 'Roraima', Water Milfoil

Myriophyllum Aquaticum 'Roraima', also known as Watermilfoil, is a beautiful cultivar that is particularly well-suited for larger aquariums. It is a fine-leafed aquatic stem plant that stands out easily with its vivid color and feathery texture. When grown in optimal conditions, the submerged form displays shades ranging from orange and yellow to deep brownish-red tones that are sure to contrast well with a variety of aquarium plant colors. It is a fast-growing variety and on the easier side when compared to other plants in the same family. Its many whorls provides a great effect in the background when planted in groups and it will tolerate a wide range of parameters.

While Roraima has no strict care requirements and will grow even in medium-hard water and without supplemental CO₂, it grows the best under strong lighting, a full supply of macro- and micronutrients in the water column, and CO2 injection. If provided, it exhibits its most vibrant colors and prolific growth. Frequent trimming is often necessary, but pruning will encourage a fuller, more compact form. Propagation is straightforward through head cuttings or by replanting trimmed side shoots.

Notes:

  • Pruning will be required to ensure other plants are not shaded from lighting.
  • Myriophyllum species makes a great midground or background filler plant choice!
  • Relatively hardy and adaptable to a range of aquarium parameters. 
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Care: Easy

products.title: Cryptocoryne Spiralis 'Jwala' UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Water Trumpet

Cryptocoryne Spiralis 'Jwala' is a striking cultivar of C. Spiralis. Characterized by narrow, slightly curled leaves, this Crypt is native to tropical regions of Asia. Like other Cryptocoryne, this aquarium plant grows in dense, rosette formations and is a great choice for adding color and texture to the midground area of a planted tank.

Its leaves are typically green, but can take on bronze to reddish tones when kept under higher light and a nutrient-rich aquarium. It grows slow to moderately and will do best in established tanks that are fully cycled and otherwise healthy. Give it moderate light, nutrient dense substrate and CO2 injection for best results. It is important to let Cryptocoryne plants become rooted before attempting to relocate or propagate. Doing this too early may result in Crypt melt!

Notes:

  • Pruning will be required to ensure other plants are not shaded from lighting.
  • Relatively hardy and adaptable to a range of aquarium parameters when established.
  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants. They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting.  Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.  
  • Cryptocoryne prefer not to be moved once planted. If possible, do not move them!
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Araceae
Endemic To: Borneo
Leaves: 5-10”
pH: 6-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
Co2: Not necessary but recommended
Propagation: Separate mother plant
Growth rate: Slow

products.title: UNS Reef System - Filter Sock Silencer
products.body_html:
Quiet down your reef with filter sock silencers! Sold in packs of 2, this reef accessory keeps your sump running smoothly and quietly. Filter sock silencers are designed to reduce loud splashing sounds that are caused by water entering the filter sock chamber, without compromising filtration performance.

Each silencer fits perfectly inside 4" filter sock openings, but are designed to match the Ultum Nature Systems Reef System sumps. By diffusing incoming flow, they help prevent turbulence and reduce noise, especially great if your tank is located in a common area like a living room or office setting. Built for function and easy use, the included handle makes it easy to install and remove. In addition to silencing, these also help catch larger debris before it reaches the sock, extending the life of filter media and need to replace.

Key Features and Important Notes:

  • Sold in packs of 2
  • Reduces splashing and noise.
  • Integrated handle makes installation and removal for cleaning quick and easy.
  • Catches large debris to reduce clogging.
  • Made from durable, reef-safe materials.
  • Designed specifically for 4" Reef System filter socks.

products.title: UNS 90E - Foresta Integrated Paludarium
products.body_html:
The Ultum Nature Systems Foresta line of Integrated Paludariums is an advanced all-in-one paludarium experience meticulously designed to serve as a flourishing habitat for both aquatic and terrestrial plant species. Each unit serves as a self-contained ecosystem that provides the perfect balance of water circulation, filtration, and humidity control for the growth and development of a diverse range of aquatic and terrestrial plant species.

The system also includes the Ultum Nature Systems Atomizer, designed to consistently produce a refreshing cooling mist that creates the perfect habitat for growing specialty aquatic and terrestrial plants. This feature helps aquascapers replicate the ideal conditions for plants and creatures that thrive in higher humidity conditions and tropical environments without needing to manually spray down the enclosure. The Ultum Nature Systems Foresta line of Integrated Paludariums brings the experience of a self-sustaining rainforest into any indoor space. Unite nature and design seamlessly and create a captivating and dynamic ecosystem in your home or office.

Key Features and Important Notes

The UNS Foresta 90E Integrated Paludarium comes complete with a comprehensive set of tools to kickstart your plant cultivation journey.

  • UNS Foresta 90E Paludarium: Crafted with precision and elegance, this rimless tank houses your ecosystem. The newly-released 60E model features a larger, more horizontal footprint for increased surface area.
  • Nano Foam Stabilizing Mat: A leveling pad specifically designed to support and stabilize the Paludarium’s weight, protecting against glass fissures and cracks while ensuring overall structural integrity and resilience.
  • Atomizer Mist Maker Set: An electronic mister that consistently produces a cooling mist effect, replicating jungle-like conditions. Essential for plants that require tropical environments with high humidity.
  • Atomizer Bracket: Specifically designed to secure the Atomizer Mist Maker Set in place for a hands-free and hassle-free experience.
  • Water Pump: A powerful and efficient pump that promotes healthy water circulation and oxygenation throughout the Paludarium.
  • Outflow Pipe: Strategically placed to maintain proper water flow and avoid stagnant “dead zones”, promoting a well-circulated ecosystem.
  • Bio Sponge Filter Media: An essential component for efficient filtration, the filter media maintains pristine water quality and houses beneficial bacteria colonies.
  • Foresta Mat Plant Pads: An absorbent yet permeable pad that acts as a quality foundation for aquatic and terrestrial plants. Its material retains some amount of water while staying permeable for airflow.
  • Foresta Line - Moss Thread: A synthetic thread perfect for attaching plants to hardscape or other desired surfaces. Securely tie and anchor plants in place, preventing unwanted movement and ensuring successful rooting to the desired area.
  • Chamber Lid: Designed to conceal the Filter Chamber, the Lid creates a clean and polished appearance while keeping your filtration system hidden.

  • Total Dimensions (LxWxH): 35.43 x 22.05 x 23.62"
  • Display Dimensions (LxWxH): 34.84 x 17.64 x 23.62”
  • Chamber Dimensions (LxWxH): 34.84 x 3.43 x 23.62”
  • Display Volume: 18.84 Gallons
  • Chamber Volume: 11.74 Gallons
  • Glass Thickness: 8mm
  • Pump Max Flow Rate: 158.5 Gallons/HR
  • Max Lift: 3.6FT
  • Power Specifications: DC12V 10W
  • Glass Thickness: 8mm

  • 1x Nano Foam Stabilizing Mat

  • 1x Water Pump

products.title: Bucephalandra Aurora
products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Aurora

Bucephalandra Aurora is an easy plant, perfect for beginners. The round leaves are generally green or lime green and can range from one cm to one inch. It can be grown submerged or immersed. The plant is very slow growing, with new leaves sprouting from long, thin rhizomes. It can be propagated by cutting the rhizome and attached to driftwood and rocks using super glue gel or thread. 

Family Name: Araceae
Endemic To: Borneo
Leaves: Large, 2-4cm in size
pH: 6-8
Care: Easy
Light: Low to Medium
Co2: Not necessary but recommended
Propagation: Cut by rhizome
Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Sagittaria Pusillus
products.body_html:
Common Name: Arrowhead

Sagittaria Pusillus is another aquatic plant that is commonly used as a foreground plant in the aquarium. This particular species will grow under a variety of conditions and is relatively forgiving. It does not require CO2, but will produce quicker growth when provided with it. Appearance wise, Sagittaria Pusillus resembles a simple grass-like plant with bold green tones and chunky leaf blades. Like other foreground carpeting plants, S. Pusillus grows via runners sent throughout the substrate. When given optimal conditions, this aquatic plant will readily cover the foreground resulting in a lush carpet.

Moderate lighting is recommended to keep the plant happy. Providing intense lighting may bring out streaks of red in this plant's leaves as well. Overall, Sagittaria Pusillus is a great addition to any planted aquarium and can be considered beginner-friendly!

Specifications:
Please note, all stats are provided as general guidelines. Factors such as height, size and overall success can vary depending on care and environment.

Origin: South America

Care: Easy

Propagation: Separate runners and replant
Growth Rate: Moderate

products.title: Dipteracanthus Elastica
products.body_html:
Common name: Dipteracanthus Bubble Plant

Bubble Plant (Dipteracanthus) is a unique terrestrial plant with deep green, rounded, bubble-like leaves. Perfect for terrariums and paludariums, it thrives in good humidity and adds instant texture to any planted setup! Its compact growth and dense structure works well for any areas that need some additional coverage. Overall, the Bubble Plant is relatively easy going, making it a good choice for beginners as well. For general care, provide it with bright, indirect light, well-draining soil and avoid overwatering or allowing its to sit in standing water to prevent root rot. When mature, it will eventually begin trailing along surfaces.

DIpteracanthus can be propagated by dividing the plant and replanted into your preferred soil or growing medium.

Notes:

  • This is not an aquarium plant. Submerging it completely will result in the death of this plant.
  • Dipteracanthus Elastica works well in terrarium-like setups and should be partially shaded with good humidity.
  • Yellowing of leaves indicates overwatering.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

products.title: Persicaria Praetermissa
products.body_html:
Common Name: Persicaria Praetermissum, Polygonum sp.

Persicaria praetermissa is a unique, fast-growing stem like plant that can be added to emersed sections of your aquascape. Alternatively, it works very well in paludariums given its marginal growth in the wild. However, this plant can be grown submerged in an aquarium and will take on an entirely new look. Watch its color change from green to a mixture of red brown tones. A good option for aquarium hobbyists in search of a unique plant that can be planted many different ways.

Like many other stem plants, providing medium to high light with the addition of CO2 will improve health and growth.

Notes: 

  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its wrap. Remove the plant weight and foam surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly prep “bunched“ aquarium plants, click here. 
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Buce Motleyana Red Under Leaf is an easy plant, perfect for beginners.  It can be grown submerged or immersed. products.title: Rotala Sp. Pearl UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Rotala Pearl

Rotala sp. Pearl is a delicate and colorful variation of Rotala that's well-suited for nano tanks. Under the right conditions, it can display a mix of green, purple, and pink hues. Its compact size makes it a great choice for the midground to background areas of a planted aquarium.

Like most Rotala species, sp. Pearl grows great when planted in groups and responds well to regular trimming, which encourages bushy, branching growth. Propagation is simple and can be performed by snipping along the stem and replanting. With its relatively fast growth rate, occasional maintenance is needed to keep lower leaves from being shaded. For best results, provide CO2 injection, micro and macronutrient dosing, and a nutrient-rich aquarium soil.

Notes:

  • Rotala has fragile leaves! Be sure to handle with care. 
  • Final coloration is ultimately dependent upon the conditions this plant is kept in. This is a live plant meaning size, shape and color may vary.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Family Name: Lythraceae
Origin: Southeast Asia
Height: 4-8”
pH: 6.5-7.5
Care: Easy
Light: High
Propagation: Cuttings
Growth Rate: Fast

products.title: Rotala Macrandra Mini Butterfly UNS Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Butterfly Mini, Rotala Macrandra

Rotala macrandra is a striking mid- to background stem plant known for its bold red coloration, offering a dramatic contrast to typical green foliage. It’s considered a more demanding plant, requiring high light, rich nutrients, and CO2 to do well. Without adequate lighting, the lower leaves may melt away.

In its emersed form, the leaves tend to grow horizontally and remain green. Submerged, it grows upright and develops deep red-magenta tones. With the right conditions, Rotala macrandra grows quickly and displays its best color when nitrates are kept low and phosphate, iron, and micronutrients are maintained at higher levels. Pale leaves or white markings are signs of iron deficiency. It prefers soft, slightly acidic water and CO₂ levels between 15–25 ppm.

To propagate, trim healthy tops and replant it into the substrate. When planting, make sure you give each stem enough space to prevent light from being blocked at the base. Regular pruning encourages fuller, bushier growth.

Notes:

  • Rotala has fragile leaves! Be sure to handle with care. 
  • Final coloration is ultimately dependent upon the conditions this plant is kept in. This is a live plant meaning size, shape and color may vary.
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

Origin: India
Height: 8-11”
pH: 5-7.5
Care: Difficult
Light: High
Co2: Required
Propagation: Trim side shoot and plant into substrate
Growth rate: Fast

Like many other stem plants, providing medium to high light with the addition of CO2 will optimize health and growth. It can be grown emersed or submerged, making it a great addition to any planted aquarium, terrarium or paludarium.

Notes: 

  • Alternanthera provides great coverage for sensitive fauna species and is a relatively easy choice for beginners who are looking for colored aquarium plants.

products.body_html:

Bucephalandra Velvet is a beautiful farmed variant of Bucephalandra. It features leaves that are light brown-red when younger that change to a deep brownish-green coloration when matured. Buce Velvet is an easy plant that grows quite densely and can also be grown emersed or submerged in an aquarium, terrarium or paludarium. Like all buce species, it's a great choice for beginners due to its ability to adapt to a wide range of water parameters. This plant is a very slow grower and sprouts new leaves from long, thin rhizomes.  It can be propagated by cutting the rhizome and attached to driftwood and rocks using super glue gel or thread.

products.body_html:
Common Name: Bucephalandra Lamandau Purple

Bucephalandra Lamandau Purple is a compact, miniature variation within the Bucephalandra family. It features remarkably tiny leaves displaying an array of deep purplish hues. It can be grown submerged or emersed. Despite its small size, it's highly prized for its pronounced iridescent coloration with appropriate care and when placed under aquarium LED lighting. This variant is an excellent choice for nano planted tanks, possibly ranking as the smallest among known Bucephalandra variations in the planted aquarium hobby.

As an epiphytic plant, Bucephalandra generates new growth from its rhizome. Optimal results are achieved for Bucephalandra in low to medium lighting conditions, fostering dense growth when provided with favorable circumstances. Faster and more robust growth can be encouraged through CO2 injection, adequate water flow, and consistent tank upkeep. When planting, don't cover the stem and rhizome to prevent plant decay and deterioration. While its care is the same as other popular aquatic species like Anubias and Java Ferns, Bucephalandra is less tolerant of abrupt parameter changes.

Samolus parviflorus 'Red' is a hardy and colorful aquatic stem plant that's perfect for your next aquascape! products.title: Assorted Hard Water Lily Pack
products.body_html:
Common Name: Water Lily
This tropical & hard water lily pack includes 5 assorted species (grower's choice) and is based on availability.

Water Lilies are a beautiful edition to any indoor or outdoor pond. The round leaves sit on the surface of the water and are supported by a long stalk. The leaves provide great coverage for fish and will help keep the water cool. Lily bulbs start producing leaves in early spring and will produce flowers throughout the summer months. These flowers will last 3-5 days and can range from yellow, pink, white, or orange in color. A seed pod will begin to form after the petals have fallen. Since seed pods take a lot of energy, it is recommended to cut the flower stalk if possible.

Hardy Lily Bulbs can survive the through the winter as long as the bulb does not freeze over. If needed they can be taken out of the water and kept in a bag full of damp sand and peat moss at 50-55 F. Ideal growing conditions include full sun and a nutrient rich substrate. Fertilizer tabs can be placed in the soil yearly to replenish the substrate as needed. Rhizomes should be planted at a 45 degree angle and only be partially covered. It is important to keep the growing tip above the substrate. Plants can be grown 1-2 feet below the water surface. Rhizomes can be divided as a form of propagation. 

Notes:

  • Water Lilies are aquatic plants, and will not survive in terrarium environments
  • Water Lilies should be overwintered indoors in freezing conditions.
  • Flower colors are random and will either be yellow, pink, white, or orange
  • Do not completely bury rhizomes as this may cause the rhizome to rot
  • Each plant is different and photos should be used as a reference
  • Proper research should be done before purchasing plants.

Family Name: Nyphaeaceae
Native: Asia
Size:  4-8inch bulbs
Ph: 6.5-7.5
Temperature: 50-85 F
Care: Easy
Light: Full Light, minimum 6 hours / day
Propagation: Herbaceous division
Growth rate: Fast

products.title: Cryptocoryne Spiralis Tiger
products.body_html:
Common Name: Crypt Spiralis 'Tiger'

Add bold texture and a natural touch to your planted tank with Cryptocoryne Spiralis 'Tiger', a striking aquarium plant characterized by its long, ribbon-like leaves adorned with tiger-like striping. Native to soft, slow-moving waters of India, this hardy Crypcotoryne plant is adaptable and can thrive in a variety of aquarium conditions, making it a versatile and visually unique choice for both beginner and experienced aquascapers. Ideal for midground to background placement, C. spiralis 'Tiger' offers height, flowing movement and beautiful contrast with its varying color palette. Intensify this aquarium plant's color by increasing lighting and providing it with quality aquarium plant nutrients, CO2 injection and good aquarium care habits. Like other crypts, this Cryptocoryne plant should be kept in a mature aquarium and allowed to establish a strong root system before moving or disturbing the plant to prevent Crypt melt!

Propagate by splitting the mother plant gently and replanting as desired. Nutrient-dense aquarium soil should be used for all Cryptocoryne plants. 

Notes:

  • Cryptocoryne plants prefer not to be moved once planted. If possible, do not move them!
  • Do not make drastic changes to the aquarium. Unstable parameters will result in melt and rotting of the aquarium plant.
  • Please be sure to remove this plant from its pot. Remove the cotton surrounding the roots and plant into a quality substrate. For instructions on how to properly plant "potted" aquarium plants, click here.
  • CO2 injection and quality aquarium soil will yield better growth.
  • Please research appropriately to ensure your plant thrives.

products.title: Cryptocoryne Pygmaea Aquatic Farmer Tissue Culture
products.body_html:
Common Name: Crypt Pygmaea

Cryptocoryne Pygmaea, sometimes known as a dwarf crypt, hails from specific islands of the Philippines. Although its name suggests it is a smaller species, this particular crypt can get fairly large when grown in a well cared for planted tank. Its emersed state is much smaller, but sufficient humidity and conditions must be provided to ensure its survival. In the wild, Cryptocoryne Pygmaea can be found along rivers emersed and submerged. Although not much is known about this Crypt, it has been said that it is generally easy to care for and undemanding making it a great choice for beginner aquarists looking to start their first planted aquarium. 

Coloration is similar to other cryptocoryne species and can vary with shades of greens and browns depending on the aquarium it is kept in. Leaf size and shapes will also vary depending wholly on the care this aquarium plant receives! Care is comparable to other Cryptocoryne species, Java Ferns, Anubias and Bucephalandra.

Notes:﻿

  • Tissue culture plants are grown in vitro from a state of the art laboratory specialized in propagating aquatic plants.  They provide the best option for those who want to ensure that absolutely zero unwanted pests are introduced into the aquarium.  
  • Remove tissue culture gel before planting.  Simply wash the gel away or soak the plant in water and squeeze the gel off with your fingers.

When kept in a nutrient-rich substrate such as Controsoil, this plant will really take off and fill in your aquarium.

Crypt Affinis is an uncommon type of crypt that use to be extremely popular within the hobby. Overall growth will be dependent on the environment it is placed in. Naturally, Crypt Affinis Red can be found growing in the wild of West Malaysia in sandy areas or along river banks. Like other crypts, Cryptocoryne Affinis Red is great for beginner hobbyists due to its ease in keeping and low care requirements. products.title: Anubias Emerald Bloom
products.body_html:
Common Name: Anubias Emerald Bloom

Anubias Emerald Bloom is another new variation of Anubias plants. This aquarium plant features large, heart-shaped foliage that are a rich green color. Like other Anubias species, Anubias Emerald Bloom is relatively hardy and easy to care for making it a great choice for beginners and seasoned hobbyists alike. This Anubias variation can be planted in the aquarium or in emersed terrarium setups. Its broad leaves offer a great way to fill in additional space between hardscape gaps.

Anubias plants will grow best when the rhizome is attached to a hard surface such as aquarium driftwood or stones. Anubias species have low to medium light requirements and will benefit from regular fertilization along with CO2 injection. While CO2 is not necessary, it will promote faster growth and more robust leaves. Since Anubias are slow growers, its leaves are susceptible to algae growth if placed under lighting that is too strong or if maintenance is not consistent.

Propagation is easy and straightforward. To propagate Anubias, simply cut or pull apart rhizomes to be replanted or reattached. Make sure to keep the rhizomes above your substrate or the plant has the potential to rot.  

Anubias Specifications
Please note, all stats are provided as general guidelines. Factors such as height, size and overall success can vary depending on care and environment.

products.body_html:
**Note: All livestock orders will come carefully packaged with solid wall Styrofoam insulation and shipped via 1-day shipping only. No extra insulation needs to be added to your cart for your livestock.

Medaka ricefish are a hardy and adaptable freshwater species that works well in community aquariums, ponds and planted tanks!

The White Medaka is a species of Japanese Ricefish. Medaka are popular in pond-style tanks and provide a unique top-down view for the hobbyist to enjoy. Like other species of Ricefish, White Medakas are very hardy and adaptable to a wide variety of aquarium parameters and salinity levels, making them a solid beginner aquarium fish. 

Ricefish are peaceful and should be kept in aquariums with other peaceful fish and larger peaceful invertebrates. Ricefish are social and should be kept in schools of 6 or more for good health and livelihood. Ricefish do have the potential to jump, so it is recommended that coverage be available if a well-fitting lid is not being used. Like most species of aquarium fauna, the White Medaka Ricefish will appreciate the addition of live aquarium plants to the tank as well.

Ricefish are omnivorous and will eat a wide variety of fish food. High-quality flakes, pellets, live or frozen will be readily accepted by Ricefish. 

Medaka Ricefish Care

products.title: CO2 Solenoid - Spring Switch Replacement
products.body_html:
This listing includes a replacement piece for the solenoid ONLY.

If bubbles continue to diffuse after the power is turned off, this replacement part ensures your solenoid functions properly and fully shuts off.

A solenoid is what will turn your regulator on and off. This is an integral part of an effective regulator because leaving CO2 on in your aquarium will lead to PH fluctuations. The solenoid gives you the ability to plug it into a timer to avoid PH fluctuations that may harm other organisms in your aquarium. This replacement part is compatible with both UNS CO2 Regulator models.

Learn more about CO2 systems and its components here!
